Protecting Vulnerable Women & Children.

Campaign against female genital mutilation, child labour, and human trafficking in Nigeria.

Get Involved Learn More
Say No to Child Trafficking Say No to FGM Say No to Rape

Who We Are

Gender Frontier Development Initiatives (GFDI) is a grassroots advocacy organization committed to defending the human rights of vulnerable women and children in Nigeria. By confronting systemic abuse and harmful traditional practices head-on, we work to protect those most at risk in our communities.

Our campaigns directly challenge female genital mutilation, child labour, and human trafficking—bringing awareness, intervening in crisis situations, and promoting education as a pathway to empowerment. We believe that every child deserves a safe environment to grow, and every woman deserves the autonomy to shape her own future without fear of violence or exploitation.

The Issues We Fight

Our core campaigns address critical human rights violations.

Female Genital Mutilation

We actively campaign against FGM, a severely harmful practice that violates the human rights, health, and physical integrity of girls and women. Our work focuses on community education and advocating for zero tolerance.

Say No to Female Genital Mutilation

Child Labour

Children belong in classrooms, not in exploitative labor conditions. We intervene to protect minors forced into hazardous work environments, advocating for their right to education and a safe childhood.

Say No to Child Labour

Human Trafficking

We fight the exploitation of human beings for profit, prioritizing the protection of women and children who are deceptively lured or violently forced into modern slavery and trafficking networks.

Say No to Child Trafficking

Child Abuse

Defending the innocence and safety of children is at the core of our mission. We provide advocacy and resource pathways for victims of physical, emotional, and systemic child abuse.

Say No to Child Abuse

Rape & Gender Violence

We stand against the pervasive culture of gender-based violence, offering a voice to survivors of rape and demanding accountability, justice, and comprehensive support systems.

Say No to Rape

Take Action With Us

Real change requires a collective effort. Whether you want to report an ongoing case of abuse, volunteer your time, or support our interventions, your voice matters.

Report a Case Partner With Us
